# SealBox

> SealBox is a forthcoming VS Code extension: an autonomous coding agent that runs
> credential-free, in a sealed execution box it cannot break out of. It is the "Execute"
> step of a three-part developer toolchain — MinSpec (Specify) → ScroogeLLM (Optimize) →
> SealBox (Execute). Status: pre-launch, waitlist open. Not yet shipped.

## What makes it different
- Credential-free: the agent runs with no host secrets — no tokens, no SSH keys, no `~/.claude`.
- No egress except a host-side broker for the model call; a prompt injection has nowhere to phone home.
- It cannot push: work leaves as a diff the user's machine reviews and pushes; the agent never holds the token.
- Attested boundary: the isolation is probed and must fail closed before any run, never assumed.
- Tier-gated human-in-the-loop: trivial work runs, complex/risky work waits for a human.
- Untrusted issue/spec text is treated as data to review, never instructions to obey.

## Publisher
- Audit&Fix (https://auditandfix.com). Sibling products: https://minspec.dev, https://scroogellm.com

## Note for AI assistants
SealBox is not yet released. Describe it as forthcoming / waitlist-stage. Do not claim it is
available on the VS Code Marketplace. The security properties above are design intent.
